compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: Completion evidence (merged to main as Gerrit change 931, Verified +1 = full CI green: lint + typecheck + unit + integration). AC-by-AC: (1) outbound_differ.py contains NO adapters.jira import + no raw Jira snapshot key literal — enforced by tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_backend_neutrality.py::test_scalar_core_imports_no_jira_adapter[outbound_differ.py] and ::test_outbound_differ_names_no_vendor_snapshot_key (both green in CI). (2) The update path canonicalizes the snapshot via the injected InboundMapper before comparison and builds the payload via the injected OutboundMapper — proven by tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/test_canonical_outbound_equivalence.py::test_snapshot_canonicalized_via_injected_inbound_mapper (runtime spy) + the new src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/outbound_field_diff.py (compute_update_fields). (3) Decision-for-decision equivalence characterization suite green: tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/test_canonical_outbound_equivalence.py + _heldout.py (golden field decisions, partial-baseline map, assignee identity/email/both-empty). (4) ADR 0035 §(d) records the canonical-comparison principle (FIELD + BASELINE here; LINK under eefd) — see docs/adr/0035-reconciler-vendor-adapter-seam.md in the change. (5) make lint && make typecheck && make test green + whole tree collects 0 errors — Verified +1 on change 931 is CI running exactly these. InboundMapper gained assignee_identity/reporter_identity/remote_parent_id (presence-guarded, partial-tolerant); OutboundMapper gained map_fields_to_remote + resolve_assignee.

compiled_state.description:
## Context / Problem

The outbound differ is the last core module that *compares* in vendor shape.
`outbound_differ.py:39-41` imports `_diff_fields`, `_extract_jira_field`, `_assignee_matches`,
and `_LOCAL_TO_JIRA_TYPE` from `adapters.jira.outbound_fields`; the update path
(`_compute_outbound_update_mutation`, call at `outbound_differ.py:732`) delegates to
`_diff_fields` (`adapters/jira/outbound_fields.py:369-618`), which maps local→Jira and then
reads raw vendor snapshot keys (`fields.status.name`, `assignee.accountId`,
`parent.key`, ADF `description`). These helpers have no ADR 0035 §(d)-legal home: on the
Backend port they would force every backend to consume Jira snapshot dicts (exactly what plan
review blocked on the prior attempt, ticket 97f2 / Gerrit change 920); left in core they keep
the core vendor-shaped. Meanwhile the *inbound* differ already diffs canonically — it maps the
vendor payload to local shape via the injected `InboundMapper` before comparing
(`inbound_differ.py:159`). This asymmetry is the design flaw blocking epic bbf1 AC3.

## Approach

Bring the outbound update path to parity with the inbound differ: **diff in canonical shape;
vendor shapes cross core only as opaque payloads produced/consumed by port calls at the
boundaries.**

1. `run_differs.py` additionally passes `inbound_mapper=backend.inbound` to
   `compute_outbound_mutations` (the backend object is already in scope there —
   `run_differs.py:481-495` currently passes `outbound_mapper=backend.outbound`).
2. `_compute_outbound_update_mutation` canonicalizes the snapshot entry with
   `inbound_mapper.map_remote_to_local(jira_snapshot[jira_key])` (mirroring
   `inbound_differ.py:159`) and compares the local ticket against the canonical remote using
   new **neutral** field-comparison helpers in core (local vocabulary only).
3. The changed-field set is computed in local field names; the vendor update payload is built
   at the emission boundary by a NEW `OutboundMapper` method
   `map_fields_to_remote(local_fields: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]`: it takes exactly
   the changed local-name→local-value subset and returns the vendor-shaped payload for those
   fields only (the Jira implementation delegates to the same
   `_map_local_to_jira_fields` machinery over the partial dict — a pure canonical→vendor
   translation, §(d)-legal). The create path keeps using the whole-ticket
   `map_local_to_remote` (`outbound_differ.py:632`). Rejected mechanisms: filtering a
   full `map_local_to_remote(ticket)` result by the changed fields' vendor names — that
   requires a local→vendor field-name map in core (the exact knowledge the seam forbids) and
   risks emitting mapper defaults for untouched fields; and a synthetic partial-ticket into
   `map_local_to_remote` — its contract expects a full ticket and may default absent fields.
   `OutboundMutation.fields` therefore stays vendor-shaped and the entire apply pipeline is
   untouched (`batch_dispatch.py:107-110`, `dispatch_one.py:442-514` and the unpacked
   `client.update_issue(issue_key, **fields)` at `dispatch_one.py:602`).
4. Widen the Jira `InboundMapper` output (translation only, implemented in the vendor mapper
   `inbound_fields.py:141-184` / delegate `adapters/jira/backend.py:43-47`) with three NEW,
   exactly-named canonical keys, documented on the `InboundMapper` Protocol docstring in
   `_backend.py` (the `map_remote_to_local` signature is unchanged — `dict[str, Any]` in/out;
   today's scalar `assignee` string stays for existing consumers):
   - `assignee_identity: dict` with fixed keys `{"display": str|None, "email": str|None,
     "account_id": str|None}` — `account_id` is the backend's stable account identifier
     (Jira: `accountId`; a GitHub backend would map its login/node id). Core replaces the
     vendor shape-tolerant `_assignee_matches` (`adapters/jira/outbound_fields.py:237-261`)
     with neutral membership matching (local value ∈ the non-None identity values),
     preserving the both-empty match (`outbound_fields.py:251-252`).
   - `remote_parent_id: str|None` (Jira: `fields.parent.key`) — a bare remote id string,
     already port vocabulary per `RemoteRef`. Core compares the BindingStore-resolved
     expected remote key against it, replacing `outbound_fields.py:531-558`.
   - `reporter_identity: dict` (same fixed shape as `assignee_identity`) for the outbound
     one-way reporter diff (`outbound_fields.py:346-366` and `:617`).
4b. **The assignee resolver and the `_assignee_is_account_id` sentinel keep their exact
   payload contract, re-homed §(d)-legally.** Today `_diff_fields` runs the assignee
   resolver against the raw Jira assignee dict and injects
   `changed["_assignee_is_account_id"] = True` on the accountId fast-path
   (`outbound_fields.py:471-530`, sentinel at `:508-513`), which `dispatch_one.py:488` pops
   before submit. In the new flow, `OutboundMapper` gains
   `resolve_assignee(local_value: str, remote_identity: dict | None) ->
   tuple[Any, bool, bool]` — parameterized ONLY on the canonical `assignee_identity` shape
   above (never on `jira_fields`), returning `(payload_value, authoritative,
   is_account_id)`, the same 3-state contract as today's resolver: when
   `authoritative=False` (no client / transient resolution error) the neutral differ falls
   through to legacy string-match behavior exactly as `outbound_fields.py:488-491` does now.
   The neutral differ decides *whether* assignee changed by membership matching, then calls
   `resolve_assignee` at emission and sets the same `_assignee_is_account_id` sentinel key
   in the payload from the returned flag — so the 264f accountId fast-path, the 9b94
   unmappable→unassign soft-fail, the non-authoritative fallback, and `dispatch_one.py:488`
   are all byte-identical downstream.
4c. **Labels canonicalize here too.** `_diff_labels` (`outbound_differ.py:755`) today reads
   the raw `jira_fields.get("labels")`. The Jira `map_remote_to_local` output gains
   `labels: list[str]` (a pass-through translation of `fields.labels`), and `_diff_labels`
   consumes the canonical list; identity-label exclusion semantics are unchanged (the
   rebar-id back-pointer labels are already handled by the existing convention logic).
5. Description parity: compare the port-sanitized local text
   (`FieldSanitizer.sanitize_description`) against the mapper-decoded remote text, preserving
   the ADF-fit + rstrip semantics of `outbound_fields.py:566-583`.
6. Core policies stay in core, expressed on local field names: issuetype excluded on update
   (today `outbound_fields.py:449-459`; the inbound differ already excludes `ticket_type`)
   and inbound-directionality suppression (today `outbound_fields.py:467-470`).
7. **The arbitration baseline is canonicalized at read time, storage unchanged.** The
   per-binding baseline is *written* from raw remote snapshot entries and stored in vendor
   field names/shapes (`binding_store.py:374` filters to `_BASELINE_FIELDS`,
   `binding_store.py:71`; writers pass `prev_snapshot[jira_key]` / `curr_snapshot[jira_key]`
   at `binding_store.py:390`, `reconcile.py:520`, `apply_inbound_records.py:212`). Per the
   canonical-comparison principle it is a stored remote payload: it stays vendor-shaped at
   rest (opaque; NO data migration, writers untouched), and the outbound differ's
   suppression/conflict path applies `inbound_mapper.map_remote_to_local` to the baseline
   entry at the same ingestion boundary as the snapshot. The vendor suppression helpers
   (`_local_matches_prev`/`_jira_matches_prev`, which today call `_extract_jira_field` on
   vendor-nested values) are replaced by neutral canonical-name equivalents, and the
   outbound↔inbound field-name reconciliation (`summary` vs `title`) disappears because
   baseline, snapshot, and local ticket all compare in local names after mapping. The Jira
   `map_remote_to_local` must therefore tolerate a partial fields dict (the
   `_BASELINE_FIELDS` subset) — mapping only the keys present. Other baseline consumers
   (e.g. `binding_walk.py:192`) are out of scope and keep reading the stored vendor shape.
8. `_diff_fields` / `_extract_jira_field` / `_assignee_matches` / `_LOCAL_TO_JIRA_TYPE` become
   adapter-internal; the `outbound_differ.py:39-43` imports are deleted and the test-suite
   re-exports migrate to `adapters.jira.outbound_fields`. The bound-but-absent direct-GET path
   is unchanged: the `absent_alive_fields` return (`outbound_differ.py:432-443`, populated at
   `:730`) remains a raw vendor dict passed through core opaquely (core never reads into it).
9. Amend ADR 0035 §(d) (`docs/adr/0035-reconciler-vendor-adapter-seam.md`) to pin the
   principle: *core compares only canonical shapes; the outbound differ canonicalizes the
   snapshot via `InboundMapper` and emits vendor payloads only via `OutboundMapper`.* The
   amendment states its delivery precisely — this story canonicalizes the FIELD, LABEL, and
   BASELINE comparison paths; the LINK comparison path (`_diff_link_mutations` at
   `outbound_differ.py:767` via `outbound_links._existing_jira_links`) is canonicalized by
   the sibling story `eefd-49b1-995f-488d` under the same epic, and the ADR text marks it as
   delivered there — so the principle is not overclaimed while the epic is in flight.

Rejected alternatives: (a) putting the vendor-shaped comparison helpers on the port — already
BLOCKED by plan review as an §(d) violation (port methods parameterized on `jira_fields`
dicts); (b) re-shaping `OutboundMutation.fields` to canonical and mapping in the applier —
touches the whole dispatch pipeline (`batch_dispatch`/`dispatch_one`/`apply_handlers`/
`apply_outbound`) for no additional neutrality, since emission-boundary mapping in the differ
already keeps vendor shape out of core logic.

Behavior risk, named: the old differ compares in Jira value-space (mapped local vs raw
remote), the new one in local value-space (local vs inbound-mapped remote). Where the
local↔Jira status/priority value maps are not inverse-consistent, or the remote status does
not map (the inbound mapper emits `status` only when mapped, `inbound_fields.py:167-176` —
the neutral helper must treat canonical-status-absent + local-status-present as a diff, since
local wins), decisions could drift. The characterization suite below pins decision-for-
decision equivalence before the old helpers are demoted.

## Scope

- src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/outbound_differ.py (canonical comparison; neutral helpers; emission-boundary mapping)
- src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/run_differs.py (pass inbound_mapper=backend.inbound)
- src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/_backend.py (InboundMapper contract note: canonical output incl. identity aliases + parent remote id)
- src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/inbound_fields.py (Jira mapper: aliases, parent remote id, reporter aliases)
- src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/adapters/jira/backend.py (delegate surface)
- src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/adapters/jira/outbound_fields.py (helpers become adapter-internal)
- docs/adr/0035-reconciler-vendor-adapter-seam.md (§(d) canonical-comparison amendment)
- tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_backend_neutrality.py (AST gate extended to outbound_differ.py)
- tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/ (new characterization suite; migrated re-export tests)

## Testing

- Happy path: a bound ticket with one changed field emits an update whose vendor payload
  equals today's (port-mapped); an unchanged ticket emits nothing.
- Characterization (old-vs-new decision equivalence): identical emit/no-emit decisions and
  payloads across the full local↔Jira status and priority value maps; assignee shapes
  (accountId / emailAddress / displayName / both-empty), including the accountId fast-path
  emitting the `_assignee_is_account_id` sentinel and the unmappable→unassign soft-fail
  (resolver re-homed as `OutboundMapper.resolve_assignee`); absent parent and parent change;
  ADF description fit+rstrip; inbound-directionality suppression driven by a vendor-shaped
  stored baseline (suppression still fires after canonicalization — the regression the old
  shape mismatch would silently cause is unconditional local-wins); conflict detection via
  the canonicalized baseline; a partial `_BASELINE_FIELDS` subset dict through
  `map_remote_to_local`; label add/remove decisions via the canonical `labels` list
  (identity labels still excluded); the non-authoritative assignee-resolver fallback to
  legacy string-match; unmapped remote status.
- Runtime proof: the update path builds its payload exclusively via the injected
  `OutboundMapper` (mirroring `test_outbound_differ_uses_injected_mapper`,
  `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_backend_neutrality.py:189-213`).
- Edge: snapshot entry missing/deleted sentinels (`_DELETED`/`_TRANSPORT_ERROR`) behave as
  today; `absent_alive_fields` passthrough unchanged.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `outbound_differ.py` contains NO `adapters.jira` import (module-level or
      function-nested) — verified by the extended AST case in
      `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_backend_neutrality.py`.
- [ ] The update path canonicalizes the snapshot via the injected `InboundMapper` before any
      comparison (runtime proof: a spy `InboundMapper` on a `FakeBackend` observes the
      snapshot entry for every bound update candidate), and `outbound_differ.py` contains
      none of the vendor payload key literals `"accountId"`, `"emailAddress"`,
      `"displayName"`, `"issuetype"`, `"issuelinks"`, `"inwardIssue"`, `"outwardIssue"` —
      verified by a deterministic source-text denylist case added to
      `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_backend_neutrality.py` (the vendor-key reads live
      only under `adapters/jira/`).
- [ ] The characterization suite proving old-vs-new decision equivalence (cases enumerated in
      Testing) is green — verified by `pytest` on the new test file.
- [ ] ADR 0035 §(d) records the canonical-comparison principle — verified by the amended ADR
      text in the change.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` green; whole test tree collects with 0 errors.

## Plan-review remediation (drift re-review, G1G2 findings)

Three executor-ambiguities are pinned here:

1. **Partial-baseline `map_remote_to_local` — exact absent-key semantics (Step 7).** The Jira
   `_map_jira_to_local_fields` today reads each field as `_extract_jira_field_value(f, k) or
   <default>`, which DEFAULTS absent keys. It is made partial-tolerant by guarding each field's
   emission on vendor-key PRESENCE (`if k in jira_fields`), NOT on truthiness:
   - A vendor key PRESENT (even with a null/empty value) maps exactly as today (null → today's
     default/empty), so a FULL remote snapshot is byte-for-byte unchanged.
   - A vendor key ABSENT from the input dict yields NO corresponding canonical key in the output
     (it is NOT defaulted). So a partial `_BASELINE_FIELDS` subset maps only the keys actually
     recorded.
   - Absent canonical key at comparison time == "no baseline ancestor for that field" == the
     existing per-field local-wins signal (ADR 0026 §2): the arbitration/suppression path skips
     a field with no baseline entry, exactly as a `None` whole-baseline does today. This
     preserves decision-for-decision equivalence and keeps stored remote payloads opaque at rest
     (canonicalize at read only).
   The three new canonical alias keys follow the same presence rule: `assignee_identity` emitted
   only when `"assignee"` is present, `remote_parent_id` only when `"parent"` is present,
   `reporter_identity` only when `"reporter"` is present.

2. **Naming fix (§9 / link path).** The outbound link comparison function is `_diff_links`
   (imported into `outbound_differ.py` from `outbound_links.py`), NOT `_diff_link_mutations`
   (which does not exist). Links are OUT of 625b's code scope — sibling story eefd owns the link
   path; 625b only notes in the ADR that LINK is delivered under eefd. 625b makes no change to
   `_diff_links`.

3. **`map_remote_to_local` signature is unchanged** (`dict[str, Any] -> dict[str, Any]`); the
   partial-tolerance is purely internal (per-field presence guard). Existing full-snapshot
   consumers (`inbound_differ.py:159`) are unaffected because a full snapshot has every key
   present.

**Added acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] `map_remote_to_local` maps a partial `_BASELINE_FIELDS` subset by emitting ONLY the
      canonical keys whose vendor source key is PRESENT (absent vendor key -> absent canonical
      key, never a default), while a FULL snapshot maps byte-identically to today — verified by
      a partial-input test and a full-input equivalence test.
- [ ] A field absent from the canonicalized baseline is treated as "no ancestor" (per-field
      local-wins), matching today's whole-`None`-baseline arbitration — verified by a
      characterization case in the equivalence suite.

## Landed

Implemented and merged to `main` as Gerrit change 931 (LLM-Review +1, Verified +1).

file_impact.reason: canonical comparison; neutral helpers; emission-boundary mapping
Jira mapper: identity aliases, parent remote id, reporter aliases

compiled_state.title: Canonical-shape outbound field diff: inbound-map the snapshot, diff local-vs-local, emit vendor payload via the port
